method: Batch Active Learning by Diverse Gradient Embedding (BADGE)
title: Batch Active Learning by Diverse Gradient Embedding (BADGE)
text_0: **Idea**: BADGE selects a batch by running k-means++ on per-sample gradient embeddings, which combine uncertainty and diversity. For each unlabeled sample, it forms the gradient of the cross-entropy loss with respect to the last linear layer using the model’s pseudo-label. Large gradient norms indicate uncertainty, while k-means++ spreads selections to avoid redundancy.
